title: Preparation of 5M Guanidine thiocyanate L6 Inactivation Buffer
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"3tmon","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"35pkg","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":[]},{"key":"6263h","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"apg4s","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":[]},{"key":"6v73p","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"b0jvk","text":"Purpose of examination / Clinical relevance","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":43}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4njkk","text":"At the end of 2019, several pneumonia cases were reported in Wuhan, China and the pathogen was confirmed as a new viral strain. World Health organization has named the newly identified coronavirus as 2019-nCoV, also known as COVID19. The disease developed into a dangerous pandemic, posing major challenges to the NHS. Although more research is necessary to better understand the virus, in response to the emergency, simple and rapid testing is essential to identify the virus in infected individuals. This will aid the implementation of efficient interventions to contain the spread, and distinguish healthcare workers who have been infected, and are required to self-isolate, from those showing similar symptoms but which are not 2019-nCoV associated. The latter category may continue to work, alleviating stress on hard-pressed healthcare resources. 2019-nCoV is an RNA virus, and the diagnostic tests detect viral RNA in swabs from patient airways using a reverse transcriptase PCR assay. Samples are submitted to HSL, an accredited reporting laboratory, and transferred to the Crick for testing.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7a2c2","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"f6j2f","text":"Principles of Examination","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":25}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3knv4","text":"This procedure involves the preparation of L6 5M Guanidine thiocyanate virus inactivation buffer for the viral inactivation protocol.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":2,"offset":105,"length":18}],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"notes","mutability":"","data":{"blocks":[{"data":[],"depth":0,"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"length":38,"offset":29}],"inlineStyleRanges":[],"key":"7ngeq","text":"This protocol is part of the Crick COVID-19 RT-PCR Testing Pipeline collection.","type":"unstyled"}],"entityMap":{"0":{"data":{"guid":"2F7F2D5096F111EABE71C340D6BFA936","url":"https://www.protocols.io/private/9BA5EEFF847911EA9B0C0242AC110002"},"mutability":"","type":"link"}}}},"1":{"type":"safety","mutability":"","data":{"blocks":[{"data":[],"depth":0,"entityRanges":[],"inlineStyleRanges":[{"length":101,"offset":0,"style":"bold"}],"key":"33p8b","text":"THIS DOCUMENT MUST BE READ AND UNDERSTOOD BY STAFF USING IT AND DOCUMENTED EVIDENCE PROVIDED THEREOF.","type":"unstyled"}],"entityMap":{}}},"2":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"4959FB6096F111EABE71C340D6BFA936","url":"https://www.protocols.io/private/E84AE35681AE11EABF5D0242AC110002"}}}}

All sharps are placed in a sharps container prior to incineration.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"safety","mutability":"","data":{"blocks":[{"data":[],"depth":0,"entityRanges":[],"inlineStyleRanges":[{"length":101,"offset":0,"style":"bold"}],"key":"33p8b","text":"THIS DOCUMENT MUST BE READ AND UNDERSTOOD BY STAFF USING IT AND DOCUMENTED EVIDENCE PROVIDED THEREOF.","type":"unstyled"}],"entityMap":{}}}}}

steps: Please select between the recipes for 1 L, 2 L, 4.3 L and 5.4 L L6 5 M Guanidine thiocyanate Inactivation buffer . Upon contact with acids, GuSCN can produce a toxic gas (HCN). As a precaution, GuSCN-containing buffers are prepared in a fume hood.
Weigh out 600 g, GuSCN and add to a 1 L beaker.
Measure out 500 mL, 0.1 M Tris HCl and add to beaker.
Measure out 110 mL, 0.2 M EDTA , pH 8.0 and add to beaker. Stir and heat to 60 °C if required.
Once GuSCN is dissolved, add 13 mL, Triton X-100 to beaker and stir well.
Vacuum filter into sterile bottles.
Label with batch and date.
Store L6 inactivation buffer in dark at solution at Room temperature ( 15 °C – 25 °C ).
Weigh out 1200 g, GuSCN and add to a 2 L beaker.
Measure out 1000 mL, 0.1 M Tris HCl and add to beaker.
Measure out 220 mL, 0.2 M EDTA , pH 8.0 and add to beaker and heat to 60 °C if required.
Once GuSCN is dissolved, add 26 mL, Triton X-100 to beaker and stirr well.
Vacuum filter into sterile bottles.
Label with batch and date.
Store L6 inactivation buffer in dark at solution at Room temperature ( 15 °C – 25 °C ).

Weigh out 2.4 kg, GuSCN in aliquots using a 3 L beaker and carefully add to 5 L beaker whilst stirring and heating to 60 °C . Rinse 3 L beaker with 0.4 L, 0.1 M Tris HCl . Stir manually with sterile 10 ml pipette if required.
Measure out 1.6 L, 0.1 M Tris HCl and add to 5 L beaker.
Vacuum filter into sterile bottles.
Label with batch and date.
Store L6 inactivation buffer in dark at solution at Room temperature ( 15 °C – 25 °C ).
Measure out 440 mL, 0.2 M EDTA , pH 8.0 and add to beaker.
Within a tissue culture hood cupboard, aliquot 1 mL, 5 M guanidine thiocyanate into 2 ml tubes and replace lids.
Label with batch and date and store in dark at Room temperature .
After the aliquoting is complete, change gloves.
Place tubes in a box clearly marked 5 M guanidine thiocyanate L6 Lysis buffer .

Clean hood, dispose of waste and switch off tissue culture hood. 5 M Guanidine thiocyanate is stable at Room temperature for at least three weeks . (Boom et al, J Clin Microbiol 1990. 28, 3, 485-503 . Commercial stocks have shelf life of 18 months)
Within a tissue culture hood cupboard, aliquot 1 mL, 5 M guanidine thiocyanate into 2 ml tubes and replace lids.
Label with batch and date and store in dark at Room temperature .
After the aliquoting is complete, change gloves.
Place tubes in a box clearly marked 5 M guanidine thiocyanate L6 Lysis buffer .

Clean hood, dispose of waste and switch off tissue culture hood. 5 M Guanidine thiocyanate is stable at Room temperature for at least three weeks . (Boom et al, J Clin Microbiol 1990. 28, 3, 485-503 . Commercial stocks have shelf life of 18 months)
Within a tissue culture hood cupboard, aliquot 1 mL, 5 M guanidine thiocyanate into 2 ml tubes and replace lids.
Label with batch and date and store in dark at Room temperature .
After the aliquoting is complete, change gloves.
Place tubes in a box clearly marked 5 M guanidine thiocyanate L6 Lysis buffer .

Clean hood, dispose of waste and switch off tissue culture hood. 5 M Guanidine thiocyanate is stable at Room temperature for at least three weeks . (Boom et al, J Clin Microbiol 1990. 28, 3, 485-503 . Commercial stocks have shelf life of 18 months)
Measure out 2.0 L, 0.1 M Tris HCl and add to 5 L beaker.

Weigh out 3 kg, GuSCN in aliquots using a 3 L beaker and carefully add to 5 L beaker whilst stirring and heating to 60 °C . Rinse 3 L beaker with 0.5 L, 0.1 M Tris HCl and add to 5 L beaker. Stir manually with sterile 10 ml pipette if required.
Measure out 550 mL, 0.2 M EDTA , pH 8.0 and add to bottle.
Add 65 mL, Triton X-100 to bottle. Swirl well to mix.
Vacuum filter into sterile bottles.
Label with batch and date.
Store L6 inactivation buffer in dark at solution at Room temperature ( 15 °C – 25 °C ).
Within a tissue culture hood cupboard, aliquot 1 mL, 5 M guanidine thiocyanate into 2 ml tubes and replace lids.
Label with batch and date and store in dark at Room temperature .
After the aliquoting is complete, change gloves.
Place tubes in a box clearly marked 5 M guanidine thiocyanate L6 Lysis buffer .

Clean hood, dispose of waste and switch off tissue culture hood. 5 M Guanidine thiocyanate is stable at Room temperature for at least three weeks . (Boom et al, J Clin Microbiol 1990. 28, 3, 485-503 . Commercial stocks have shelf life of 18 months)
Once GuSCN is dissolved, add 52 mL, Triton X-100 to beaker. Stir well to mix.
Once GuSCN is dissolved, transfer to 5 L single use tissue culture bottle.
creator: Jerome Nicod
